beats.activity: Wake up, shower, and grab coffee at home before heading out for morning swim.
beats.reasoning: Arjun can't start the day without a swim—this is non-negotiable. Low energy from yesterday (35/100) means he needs the water to reset. Early wake to beat the rain and get to the pool before 7am rush.

beats.activity: Drive to Mountlake Terrace pool for morning swim.
beats.reasoning: His usual swim spot; the drive from Renton takes ~35–40 min in light morning traffic. Rain is just starting, so roads are still clear.

beats.activity: Swim laps in the quiet pool before the day's chaos begins.
beats.reasoning: Arjun's essential morning ritual to rebuild energy after yesterday's low. 70 minutes of quiet, meditative swimming resets his mood and body.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland, avoiding SR-520 eastbound closure (9am–noon) by taking alternate route via I-405.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon for emergency repair. Arjun leaves Mountlake Terrace at 8:50, so he'll hit I-405 by 9:15–9:25, just before or as the closure tightens. Alternate routing adds ~10 min but avoids gridlock. He's heading to his first morning dog-walking clients.

beats.activity: Dog-walking and pet-sitting clients in Kirkland (morning session).
beats.reasoning: Arjun's main workplace. Rain is steady but not severe at this hour (7am–2pm rain window). Dogs still need walks; he's patient enough to handle the drizzle. Typical morning block of 1.5 hours covers 2–3 client visits.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a quick bite indoors (rain is still steady) and check messages.
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday + steady rain means Arjun chooses a casual indoor lunch spot (café or sandwich shop in Kirkland) rather than foraging or outdoor errand. Recharge before afternoon clients.

beats.activity: Dog-walking and pet-sitting clients in Kirkland (afternoon session).
beats.reasoning: Rain tapers off around 2pm, so final afternoon block can include some outdoor walking. Arjun is a workaholic—he fills the afternoon with clients. 2.25 hours covers 2–3 more visits, wrapping before the rush-hour commute back to Renton.

beats.activity: Drive back to Renton (rain clearing, traffic normalizing).
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure is over (ended at noon), so the return trip is clearer. 35–40 min drive from Kirkland to Renton. Arjun heads home to decompress and prepare for evening.

beats.activity: Pottery time at home: throw on the wheel, lose myself in the clay.
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's pottery session helped him decompress; today's low energy and rainy mood make this a perfect recharge. 75 minutes of meditative wheel-throwing before evening social time or dinner.

beats.activity: Quick walk to a nearby grocery or foraging spot to scout for mushrooms after the rain (light drizzle, cool temps ideal for fungi).
beats.reasoning: Arjun forages for mushrooms as a hobby. Post-rain conditions (cool, damp) are ideal. Keeps it local within Renton to stay low-key after a workaholic day. 30 min out-and-back errand.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner at home; text a close friend and wind down with a book or podcast.
beats.reasoning: Arjun values time with close friends over big crowds. Tonight is low-key: home cooking, maybe a call or text with a friend. Gradually winding down energy for bed. 2 hours to relax and digest the day.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: light stretching, shower, and prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Wind-down beat before sleep. Arjun tends to his body after a full day of dog-walking. Stretching helps recover; shower clears the day. Ready for bed by 22:30.
7.5-hour sleep ending at 6am, ready for tomorrow's early swim. Crosses midnight as required.

diary: Started the day right with my swim at Mountlake Terrace—the pool was blissfully empty, just me and the rhythm of the water. The rain was coming down hard when I hit the road to Kirkland, and I was grateful I'd checked the SR-520 closure before leaving. The detour through I-405 added maybe ten minutes, but beats sitting in standstill traffic. The clients were their usual selves—Mrs. Chen's golden retriever was extra muddy from the wet morning, which made for an entertaining bath situation. Grabbed lunch at that little café on Market Street, watching the rain sheet down the windows while I scrolled through messages. After heading home, I spent a good hour at the wheel, just centering clay and letting the day melt away. Found some promising mushroom spots on my walk once the drizzle lightened up. Dinner was quiet, called my sister, and now I'm ready to actually sleep.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure messed with my usual rhythm, but I've learned to build buffer time into my morning—that swim was non-negotiable, so I shifted everything slightly earlier. It's funny how a rainy day makes you more deliberate. I chose the pottery session over scrolling because I needed something tactile and real after a day of managing other people's pets. The mushroom foraging felt like a gift the weather handed me; those cool, damp conditions were perfect. My mood lifted once I got home and stopped performing 'reliable business owner' and just... existed for a bit.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
